Off Calendar Aug-21-2020 Continued To Oct-21-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-02-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Kelly Collins, Deputy City Attorney 415-554-3914 for Defendant CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested/Discussed. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer is sustained with leave to amend.
The check-box complaint alleges that plaintiff Mariam Moasser's leg fell through a hole in a PG&E plate in a sidewalk on April 24, 2019. The Government Claims Act must be satisfied before suing the City, and compliance is treated as "an element of a valid cause of action." (Gov. Code 905, 945.4; Gong v. City of Rosemead (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 363, 374.)

Plaintiff's lawyer, Thomas Tagliarini, filed a claim against the City on September 6, 2019. However, Tagliarini left blank the claim form's key section, which calls for "the facts and circumstances of the incident" and "why you believe the City is responsible." The late-filed opposition offers no explanation for this. On September 23, 2019, the City sent Tagliarini a "NOTICE OF INSUFFICIENCY" saying: "Your client's document(s) fail to clearly state a theory of liability against the City and County of San Francisco and facts to support it." Tagliarini does not assert that he responded to the City's notice, and again offers no explanation. On October 9, 2019, the City sent Tagliarini a "NOTICE OF ACTION ON CLAIM" saying: "An investigation of your claim has revealed no liability on the part of the City and County." Once more, Tagliarini failed to respond and offers no explanation why.

The opposition makes three basic arguments. First, it says the City should have "determined Ms. Masser's theory of liability" from witnesses on its own. Unsurprisingly, no authority is cited for the notion that a city is obligated to develop a claimant's liability theories for her. Second, Tagliarini claims "substantial compliance" - an argument belied by the undisputed facts. Third, "estoppel" is asserted because the City's September 23 notice "did not state that the absence of a stated theory rendered the claim a nullity." This argument would have more force had it been made by a pro per plaintiff. Tagliarini's state bar number indicates he started practicing law more than 35 years ago.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
